input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install latest .NET SDK via winget
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# Discover available workloads dynamically
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (for Windows target)

Windows App SDK requires the Windows SDK and build tools:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or if you only want build tools:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required components:

```powershell
# Modify VS installation to include MAUI + UWP/WinUI components
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## 4. Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload installs a minimal Android SDK. To manage it explicitly:

```powershell
# Find where dotnet placed the Android SDK
dotnet android sdk info

# Or locate it manually
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
ls $androidSdk

# Discover available platforms/build-tools dynamically
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install specific components (example — pick versions from --list output)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Set Environment Variables

```powershell
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk", "User")
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME", "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\jdk\jdk-17*", "User")

# Refresh current session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
```

## 6. Create & Verify a Project

```powershell
# Create new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## 7. Dynamic Version Discovery Cheat Sheet

| What | Command |
|------|---------|
| Available .NET SDKs | `winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK` |
| Available workloads | `dotnet workload search` |
| Installed workloads | `dotnet workload list` |
| Android platforms | `sdkmanager.bat --list \| Select-String "platforms"` |
| Target frameworks | `dotnet new maui --list` then inspect `.csproj` |
| Windows SDK versions | `Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include"` |

---

**Key notes:**
- The `maui` workload pulls in `android`, `ios`, and `windows` sub-workloads automatically.
- For Windows targets, you need the Windows App SDK — Visual Studio installs this; standalone requires the [WindowsAppSDK NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK).
- Java (OpenJDK 17) is bundled with the Android workload — no separate install needed.
- Replace `net9.0` with your installed SDK major version if different.
